learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate comprehension of basic economics and finance news.
Demonstrate expertise in analysing and evaluating financial markets, instruments, and institutions.
Evaluate the validity of economic theory by contrasting it with real world data.
Analyse the implementation and impact of monetary policy in a modern economy.
Create an analysis of a course related topic and present it clearly and freely to a peer audience.

assessments.category: Participation/ Student contribution, Quiz, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessments.task_description: There will be 3 online, open book, non-invigilated tests. These tests replace the mid-semester exam and part of the final exam and will therefore be more difficult and longer than the Warm-Up Reading Quizzes. The format includes MCQ, SAQ, fill in the blanks, calculus and graphical answers. The idea is to work continuously on these tests in the workshops. Do NOT wait till due date since students used several hours for each test in previous semesters.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Penalties will be applied for late submissions as follows:
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: There will be 12 short online quizzes with 10 - 15 questions each. Each quiz will be available for several days and is not timed. The quiz questions will relate to reading exercises for future classes and / or topics from previous lectures. The exact timing of the quizzes will be announced on blackboard. Each quiz will be due in the first 15 minutes of the workshop and no extension will be granted, as these are ensuring that you have done the pre-class readings. You are strongly encouraged to do the reading and attempt the quiz BEFORE the workshop. Each quiz counts for 1% of the total. Only the best 10 quizzes count toward your final mark. This allows you to miss two quizzes without any impact on your mark.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.category: Practical/ Demonstration, Presentation, Project, Role play/ Simulation
assessments.task_description:
Student create teams early in the semester and get allocated a topic that they have to analyse using the tools from class. The task is to use the knowledge from the workshop / textbook material combined with very limited independent research to present their topic to their fellow students. A detailed task sheet / marking rubric will be provided 2 weeks before presentations start. The presentation time slot will be allocated in coordination with the teams' preference and is OUTSIDE of workshop times. This presentation also serves as practice run for the more important end of semester presentation. 

Duration:

6 minutes for a team of 3 students + 5 minutes question time (smaller / larger teams require written exception and time is adjusted proportionately, i.e. 2 minutes per student)

Format:

Students present in allocated time slots. Simply reading (e.g, notes) is not allowed. All team members shall participate equally. 

All presentations are recorded.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Presentation will be given at allocated / chosen timeslot. The performance during that presentation and Q&A is marked. The slide set submission after the presentation is just a formality required to receive the marks from the actual presentation.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.category: Creative Production/ Exhibition, Performance, Practical/ Demonstration, Presentation, Project
assessments.task_description:
Students are allowed to use the same team as for presentation 1 or change teams. Each team will create / choose a specific topic in coordination with the lecturer / tutor to analyse and present. It is your job to impress us with your work using the analytical tools learned in class. 

Duration:

12 minutes for a team of 3 students + 5 minutes question time (smaller / larger teams time is adjusted proportionately)

Format:

Students present in allocated time slots. Simply reading (e.g, notes) is not allowed. All team members shall participate equally. 

All presentations are recorded.
assessments.submission_guidelines: After the presentation, the slides are uploaded on blackboard as a formality. The marks are determined by the actual presentation.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
